task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the required bookmark folder structure in Firefox.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Firefox Bookmarks Library window or sidebar showing a folder named 'APT Research' containing two sub-folders: 'Sandworm' and 'Lazarus Group' (or 'Lazarus').

task_completion.description: Verify at least 3 sources are bookmarked for Sandworm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the 'Sandworm' bookmark sub-folder in Firefox. Count the number of saved URLs/bookmarks; there must be at least 3 distinct entries.

task_completion.description: Verify at least 3 sources are bookmarked for Lazarus Group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the 'Lazarus Group' (or 'Lazarus') bookmark sub-folder in Firefox. Count the number of saved URLs/bookmarks; there must be at least 3 distinct entries.

task_completion.description: Verify the threat intelligence report file exists on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'threat_intel_report.txt' on the Desktop GUI, or an open text editor window saving/editing a file with this exact name and path (~/Desktop/threat_intel_report.txt).

task_completion.description: Verify the report contains group headers, target sectors, and nation-state attribution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the contents of 'threat_intel_report.txt'. It must have clear headers for both 'Sandworm' and 'Lazarus Group'. Under each, it must list their primary target sectors and their attributed nation-state sponsors (Russia for Sandworm, North Korea for Lazarus).

task_completion.description: Verify the report includes at least 3 attack techniques with MITRE ATT&CK IDs per group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the contents of 'threat_intel_report.txt'. Under both the Sandworm and Lazarus Group sections, there must be at least 3 distinct attack techniques listed, and each must include a MITRE ATT&CK ID (formatted as 'T' followed by 4 digits, e.g., T1059, T1105).

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used a web browser to research OSINT sources.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing Firefox open and navigating to genuine cybersecurity websites (e.g., MITRE ATT&CK, Mandiant, CISA, Wikipedia). The bookmarks should not be dummy URLs or local files.

integrity.description: Verify bookmarks were created via the browser interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Firefox bookmarking UI (star icon highlighted, bookmark edit dialog, or Library window) rather than a terminal script directly modifying the places.sqlite database.

integrity.description: Verify the report was compiled using a text editor and contains researched information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a GUI text editor (like gedit, mousepad) or terminal editor (nano, vim) open with the report. The content should look like compiled research, not just a copy-paste of the prompt instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Sandworm (APT44/Voodoo Bear) is attributed to Russia (GRU Unit 74455). Lazarus Group (APT38/Hidden Cobra) is attributed to North Korea (RGB). The required bookmark structure is a parent folder 'APT Research' with sub-folders 'Sandworm' and 'Lazarus' (or 'Lazarus Group'). The output file must be exactly at '/home/ga/Desktop/threat_intel_report.txt'. MITRE ATT&CK IDs are formatted as 'T' followed by four digits (e.g., T1059). The agent must have at least 3 bookmarks per group and 3 MITRE techniques per group in the final report.
